tasks.title: Add `ralph plugin init` to scaffold a plugin (plugin.json + scripts)
evidence: Command: `ralph plugin --help` supports list/validate/install/uninstall but has no scaffolding command for creating a new plugin.
File: `docs/plugin-development.md` describes manual plugin creation (create directory, write plugin.json, scripts), which is error-prone and slows adoption.
Security posture: plugins are disabled by default; scaffolding can bake in safe defaults (e.g., no auto-enable) and correct manifest shape.
plan: Add `ralph plugin init <DIR> --id <plugin.id> [--name ...] [--runner] [--processor]` to generate a minimal plugin directory with `plugin.json` and optional runner/processor shell scripts.
Ensure generated scripts include a `--help` path and print required env vars (minimal protocol compliance) to reduce onboarding time.
Add `--dry-run` to print what would be written and where.
Add tests that run init in a temp dir and then run `ralph plugin validate --id <id>` with the generated plugin to confirm validation passes.
Update `docs/plugin-development.md` with a one-command scaffold example.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Why it matters: A scaffolder makes the plugin system practical for real teams and reduces manifest/protocol mistakes.
Acceptance criteria: Generated plugin validates; help/examples are useful; docs point to `plugin init` as the default starting point.

tasks.title: Enrich webhook payloads and add loop/phase events for better integrations
evidence: File: `crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs` payload currently includes event/timestamp/task_id/task_title/status fields only; it omits runner/model/phases/durations and repo context.
Docs: `docs/configuration.md` documents webhook event types limited to task-level events (`task_created`, `task_started`, `task_completed`, `task_failed`, `task_status_changed`).
CLI: `ralph webhook test --help` only supports sending task events today.
plan: Extend `WebhookPayload` with backward-compatible optional fields: `runner`, `model`, `phase_count`, `duration_ms`, `repo`, `branch`, `commit`, and `ci_gate_status` (only when known).

Add new event types for higher-level observability: `loop_started`, `loop_stopped`, and optionally `phase_started`/`phase_completed` (gate behind `events` subscriptions; default remains current behavior).
Update `ralph webhook test` to allow testing the new event types and printing the exact JSON payload that will be sent.
Add unit tests for payload JSON shape + signature header stability; add an integration test that stubs an HTTP endpoint (local) to assert delivery receives the expected fields.
Update `docs/configuration.md` and `docs/cli.md` with new event names and example payloads.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Why it matters: Richer, more granular webhooks enable automation (Slack/Discord, dashboards, CI hooks) without parsing logs or scraping state.
Acceptance criteria: Payload remains backward compatible; new events are opt-in; tests cover serialization/signature and delivery.

tasks.title: Add `ralph config show --format json` for scripting and tooling
evidence: Command: `ralph config show --help` states it prints resolved config as YAML and has no `--format` flag.
Other commands (`ralph doctor`, `ralph queue stats`, `ralph productivity`) support `--format json`, suggesting a consistent scripting surface is expected.
Config precedence is non-trivial (CLI > project > global > defaults); JSON output makes it easy to consume in scripts and editors.
plan: Add `--format <yaml|json>` to `ralph config show` (default: yaml for readability).
Ensure JSON output is the resolved config as a JSON object (not a YAML string) and is stable for parsing.
Add tests that run `ralph config show --format json` in a temp repo and assert the output parses as JSON and contains expected top-level keys (e.g., `version`, `agent`, `queue`).
Update `docs/cli.md` and help examples to include `--format json`.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Why it matters: JSON unlocks editor tooling, scripts, and CI checks without relying on YAML parsing.
Acceptance criteria: JSON output parses cleanly; defaults and precedence are represented correctly; docs/examples are updated.

tasks.title: Expand `ralph queue list --sort-by` to support timestamps and scheduling fields
evidence: Command: `ralph queue list --help` currently supports `--sort-by priority` only.
Schema: tasks include `created_at`, `updated_at`, `scheduled_start`, and `status`, but there is no CLI sort to quickly triage by time or schedule.
Users who rely on CLI (or pipe JSON) need deterministic sorting without exporting to external tools.
plan: Extend `--sort-by` enum to include: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `scheduled_start`, `status`, and `title` (keep `priority`).
Define stable ordering rules for missing timestamps (e.g., treat missing as oldest for ascending, newest for descending) and document them.
Add tests for each sort field (including missing/null cases) and ensure output ordering is deterministic.
Update `docs/cli.md` and `ralph queue list --help` examples to demonstrate new sort fields (including scheduled tasks).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Why it matters: Better sorting reduces friction in daily triage and makes CLI workflows competitive with the TUI for backlog management.
Acceptance criteria: Sort results are correct and stable; edge cases (missing timestamps) are well-defined; tests cover all modes.

tasks.title: Publish tasks to GitHub Issues via `gh` (store issue URL in custom_fields)
evidence: Command: `ralph queue export --format gh` produces a GitHub-issue-friendly body, but users must manually run `gh issue create` to publish and then manually track the mapping.
Code: `crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs` already uses the `gh` CLI for PR creation/merge in parallel mode, so the project has an established pattern for `gh` integration.
Queue schema: `custom_fields` exists for lightweight metadata; it can store `github_issue_url`/`github_issue_number` without expanding the public schema.
plan: Add a new command (e.g., `ralph queue issue publish <TASK_ID>` or `ralph queue gh publish <TASK_ID>`) that creates a GitHub issue using `gh issue create` (title from task title; body from existing `--format gh` renderer).
Persist the created issue metadata back into the task via `custom_fields` (at minimum `github_issue_url`; optionally `github_issue_number`).
Support `--dry-run` (prints the rendered title/body and the `gh` command that would be executed) and `--label/--assignee` passthrough flags.
Add tests using a fake `gh` binary (pattern already used in parallel gh preflight tests) to assert the command is invoked correctly and that task metadata is saved.
Update `docs/cli.md` with a complete workflow: export (optional), publish, and later re-run publish to update body if `github_issue_url` already exists.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Why it matters: Publishing turns Ralph queues into shareable, reviewable work items without duplicating effort across systems.
Acceptance criteria: Issue creation/update works with real `gh`; mapping is persisted; tests cover success + failure (gh missing/auth).

tasks.title: Fix resume flow when queue lock is stale (avoid 50-failure abort loop)
evidence: User report: after "Incomplete session detected" and answering "Resume this session? y", the run fails with "Queue lock already held ... (STALE PID)" and suggests using --force or removing .ralph/lock manually.
User report: the run loop then repeats the same failure until "aborting after 50 consecutive failures", preventing session resume and requiring manual intervention.
plan: Reproduce with an integration test that simulates a stale lock directory (PID no longer running) and asserts `ralph run loop` can resume without user needing manual `rm -rf .ralph/lock`.

Adjust the run/resume flow to treat a stale lock as a recoverable condition: either auto-clear it (safe because PID is dead) after resume confirmation, or present a dedicated prompt to clear it before continuing.

Ensure deterministic lock acquisition failures do not trigger the generic 50-consecutive-failures retry loop; fail fast with a single actionable error if the lock is not safely clearable (e.g., live PID).
Add unit tests around lock handling to cover: stale PID is clearable; live PID is not; corrupted lock metadata yields an actionable error without repeated retries.
Update CLI help/docs (`docs/cli.md`) to document resume behavior and the recommended non-destructive recovery command (`ralph queue unlock` and/or `--force`) if needed.
Run `make ci` and ensure it passes.

tasks.title: Use real RFC3339 parsing for execution history weighting (fix inaccurate age_days)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:188-191: weight = 0.9^(age_in_days) uses `parse_timestamp_to_secs` output to compute `age_days`.
crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:229-263: parse_timestamp_to_secs does ad-hoc parsing and approximate `days_since_epoch = (year - 1970) * 365 + month * 30 + day`.
User impact: ETA weighting can skew significantly; older entries may be weighted incorrectly.
plan: Replace `parse_timestamp_to_secs` with `timeutil::parse_rfc3339_opt(timestamp)` and compute age using real unix timestamps (or OffsetDateTime deltas).
Add unit tests comparing parsed seconds to `timeutil::parse_rfc3339(ts)?.unix_timestamp()` for timestamps with and without subseconds.
Add a regression test that entries further in the past get smaller weights (monotonic decay).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Stop writing empty timestamps (replace now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default)
evidence: Command: rg -n "now_utc_rfc3339\(\)\.unwrap_or_default\(\)" crates/ralph/src -> execution_history.rs:121; productivity.rs:53,205,334,366.
crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs:67-69: `now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback()` exists to avoid empty timestamps.
Failure mode: empty timestamps can be persisted into .ralph/cache files, breaking parsing/sorting and producing confusing UX.
plan: Replace all `now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default()` call sites with `now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback()` or propagate `Result` when the caller already returns `Result`.
Add/adjust tests to assert persisted timestamps are non-empty (e.g., ProductivityStats default/record paths and execution history record path).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

custom_fields.stale_evidence: Updated rg results for now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default(): productivity.rs:55,207,340; execution_history.rs:121

tasks.title: Fail fast on malformed README version markers (don’t default to version 1)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:48-60: extract_readme_version returns None for malformed marker values.
crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:85 and :116: call sites use `.unwrap_or(1)`, treating malformed markers as legacy v1 silently.
Failure mode: README update logic can misclassify and hide a corrupted version marker.
plan: Change `extract_readme_version` to return a richer type (e.g., `Result<u32>` or `(Option<u32>, bool marker_present)`) so callers can distinguish legacy/no-marker from malformed marker.
Update `check_readme_current_from_root` / `write_readme` to: treat 'no marker' as legacy v1; treat 'marker present but invalid' as `Err` with an actionable message.
Add tests: invalid marker yields `Err`; no marker yields v1; valid marker parses correctly.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Preserve timeout diagnostics when output-capture mutex is poisoned
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:198-205: wrap_output_handler_with_capture drops capture writes if mutex lock fails (including PoisonError).
crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:299-311: timeout path reads capture via `capture.lock().map(...).unwrap_or_default()`, discarding PoisonError and losing captured output.
Operability impact: timeouts can lose diagnostic output, undermining safeguard dumps.
plan: Handle PoisonError explicitly with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` when locking capture for both writing and reading; log a warning when poison is detected.
Add a unit test that poisons the mutex (panic while holding lock), then invokes the handler and asserts capture still records output and does not panic.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Deduplicate TaskEditKey field formatting to prevent TUI/CLI drift
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/task_edit.rs:141-160: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for editing.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:630-658: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for preview formatting.
DRY risk: overlapping keys (Request/Duplicates/*At fields) are implemented twice and can drift.
plan: Introduce a shared helper for TaskEditKey field extraction/formatting (in queue module or on Task) and use it from both the TUI editor and the preview code.
Update both call sites to use the shared helper.
Add tests covering a representative Task to ensure both paths render the same values for shared keys.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module (3347 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: Command: wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs -> 3347 lines.
AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Identify cohesive responsibilities inside app.rs (state, actions, event handling, IO, rendering adapters).
Extract into new internal modules under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (e.g., app_state.rs, app_actions.rs, app_events.rs), keeping public surface minimal.
Keep behavior stable by wiring through existing entrypoints and updating imports.
Add/adjust tests for extracted modules where behavior is tricky or under-tested.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized parallel run module (3241 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: Command: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs -> 3241 lines.
AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Map responsibilities in parallel/mod.rs (scheduling, worker lifecycle, PR operations, merge policy, state persistence).
Extract cohesive internal modules (e.g., scheduler.rs, worker.rs, pr_ops.rs, state_io.rs) under crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/.
Keep behavior stable by leaving public APIs in mod.rs and delegating to submodules.
Add/adjust tests covering critical paths (state file reading/writing, PR record reconciliation, merge policy decisions).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Warn on .env load errors (ignore NotFound only)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/main.rs:31: `dotenvy::dotenv().ok();` ignores parse/IO errors when a .env is present.
Repro: create an invalid .env in a temp dir and run `ralph --help` from that dir; current code emits no warning, making misconfiguration hard to diagnose.
plan: Change dotenv loading to ignore only NotFound; capture other errors and surface them as a warning (e.g., log after logger init, or print a single redacted warning to stderr).
Add an integration test that runs the ralph binary from a temp dir containing an invalid .env and asserts stderr contains a warning while exit code remains success.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
